summary:
The primary route for administering midazolam includes IV, IM, IN, but not specifically prohibited orally; hence it does not fit a scenario where an alternative must exclude this method due to its inability across multiple listed options except A. Therefore, based solely from available data indicating general permissibility through various means without direct contra-indications noted especially towards oral intake among those specified choices, option \(\boxed{\text{A}}\) stands out appropriately here. 

Note how I've condensed your explanation into two concise yet comprehensive statements focusing strictly upon critical aspects pertinent to answering whether there’s specific prohibition about one particular mode over others concerning midazolam dosages. This maintains clarity while ensuring alignment closely related back toward original query posed initially within context supplied earlier too!
